response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and backend engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js depth and genuine distributed-systems exposure, but he falls critically short of the core minimum qualifications for this role. The job mandates 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project — a gap too large to bridge. His cloud experience is real (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, high-throughput microservices) but remains peripheral rather than platform-defining, and there is no evidence of a formal technical leadership role owning a team or setting architectural direction at scale.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O) demonstrates real large-scale infrastructure instincts
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, DLang, Java, C/C++, Node.js) and full ownership of the GISCollective open-source platform show architectural range and follow-through
Active knowledge-sharing record — workshops, conference talks, Refactoring Dojos — aligns with the mentoring expectations of the role
concerns: C++ experience is effectively under 1 year vs. the 8-year hard minimum, which is the primary programming language requirement for this position
No documented tenure as a formal technical lead managing engineers, setting team-level architectural standards, or owning cross-team alignment — the role's core leadership expectations are unmet
Cloud platform work is secondary and tooling-level (AWS CDK for pricing, Kubernetes for a side project) rather than building or owning a compute platform end-to-end
